title: California Voter Registration
description: Register to vote, update an existing voter registration record, or pre-register (ages 16-17) in California, administered by the California Secretary of State through the state's online voter registration system (covr.sos.ca.gov, publicly linked from registertovote.ca.gov). Unlike au/aec/voter-enrolment, which models a single national electoral roll, voter registration in the United States is state-administered — this document models California's own state-run process specifically, not a national US process. Models the wizard's classification/eligibility step, personal information (name, date and place of birth, identity-proofing via a California driver license/ID number or the last four digits of a Social Security number, ethnicity, and language preference), home and mailing address (including a no-street-address branch for applicants without a conventional street address), a previous-registration/name-change recall section, political party preference, election-materials mailing preferences, poll-worker volunteering, accessibility, and third-party-assistance disclosure, ending with a penalty-of-perjury affirmation. Does not model the confirmation/electronic-signature step that follows the application review page, and does not submit an application; the live covr.sos.ca.gov source is always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md).

source.documentRef: California Online Voter Registration wizard (live application at covr.sos.ca.gov, linked from registertovote.ca.gov's 'Register to Vote Now'); Steps 1-4 of 5 (Classification, Personal Information, Additional Information/Consent, Review) walked live via direct HTTP form submission with mock data, stopping before the final Submit action on the Review page.

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored by directly operating the live covr.sos.ca.gov wizard: fetched Step 1 (Classification) live, then walked Steps 2 (Personal Information — a single large page combining Qualifications, Personal Information, Home and Mailing Address, and Political Party Preference field groups), 3 (Additional Information/Consent), and 4 (Review, a read-only summary of the submitted data), each by POSTing well-formed form data (with the page's own anti-forgery token) directly to the wizard's /en/OnlineVoterRegistration/PostForm endpoint and reading the server's own re-rendered HTML — not a static-PDF fallback, and not a headless browser, but the same practical effect: exercising the real, live, server-validated wizard end-to-end. Stopped at the Review page without pressing the final 'Submit' action, so no real application was filed. Status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md for the full walkthrough record, the field-by-field source mapping, and an honestly-flagged server-validation quirk found along the way.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: Which of seven eligibility categories the applicant registers under. This schema models the domestic in-state civilian path (us_citizen_ca_resident) in full; the six UOCAVA/military/overseas categories share this same classification field and wizard, but were not walked in this verification pass and may surface additional fields (e.g. an overseas mailing address, unit/rank) not modeled here — see VERIFICATION.md 'Out of scope'.
fields.sourceRef: Step 1 'Classification' — 'I am one of the following', field ClassificationType

fields.name: isUSCitizen
fields.label: I am a U.S. citizen and resident of California.
fields.description: Eligibility attestation checkbox; must be checked to proceed.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Qualifications', field IsUSCitizen

fields.name: registrationChoice
fields.label: Registration/pre-registration
fields.description: Whether the applicant is registering as an adult or pre-registering as a 16- or 17-year-old (California allows pre-registration from age 16, with automatic activation to a registered voter at 18). The wizard's own date-of-birth validator enforces a minimum age of 16 regardless of this choice.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Qualifications', field RegistrationChoice — 'I am 18 or older.' / 'I am 16 or 17 and want to pre-register.'

fields.name: prefix
fields.label: Prefix
fields.description: Optional name prefix.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Legal Name', field Prefix

fields.name: firstName
fields.label: First name
fields.description: Applicant's legal first name.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Legal Name', field FirstName

fields.name: middleName
fields.label: Middle name
fields.description: Applicant's legal middle name.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Legal Name', field MiddleName

fields.name: lastName
fields.label: Last name
fields.description: Applicant's legal last name.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Legal Name', field LastName

fields.name: suffix
fields.label: Suffix
fields.description: Optional name suffix.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Legal Name', field Suffix

fields.name: emailAddress
fields.label: Email address
fields.description: Optional contact email address. The wizard's own client-side script compares this against confirmEmailAddress for a match, but this is not enforced by the server model unconditionally; a consumer SHOULD still ensure the two match when both are supplied.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field EmailAddress

fields.name: confirmEmailAddress
fields.label: Confirm email
fields.description: Repeat of emailAddress for confirmation; see emailAddress's description.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field ConfirmEmail

fields.name: phoneNumber
fields.label: Phone number
fields.description: Optional daytime phone number, digits only. The form notes phone numbers are posted at polling places on election day.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field PhoneNumber

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.description: Full date of birth. The source form renders this as three separate month/day/year dropdowns/box (fields MonthOfBirth, DayOfBirth, YearOfBirth), collapsed here to one field per this registry's comb-field convention. The wizard's own validator enforces a minimum age of 16 as of the submission date (observed acceptable birth-year range: 1901-2010 against a 2026-07-02 access date).
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', fields MonthOfBirth, DayOfBirth, YearOfBirth

fields.name: placeOfBirthType
fields.label: Place of Birth
fields.description: Whether the applicant's place of birth is a U.S. state/territory or a foreign country/region. Presented on the form with a conditional-required visual indicator, but no server-side required validation was observed when this field and its dependents (statePlaceOfBirth, countryPlaceOfBirth) were left entirely blank.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Place of Birth', field PlaceOfBirthType

fields.name: statePlaceOfBirth
fields.label: Place of birth — U.S. state/territory
fields.description: U.S. state or territory of birth. The source presents a fixed 59-item dropdown (50 states, D.C., and 8 territories); modelled as free text per this registry's convention for large geographic dropdowns (e.g. us/dos/immigrant-visa-ds260's state/country fields) rather than embedding the full option list as an enum.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field StatePlaceOfBirthId

fields.name: countryPlaceOfBirth
fields.label: Place of birth — foreign country/region
fields.description: Foreign country or region of birth. The source presents a fixed ~200-item country dropdown; modelled as free text per this registry's large-geographic-dropdown convention.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field CountryPlaceOfBirthId

fields.name: californiaId
fields.label: California driver license or California identification card number
fields.description: One letter followed by seven numeric digits. Required unless hasNoCaliforniaId is checked. New voters who supply neither this nor a Social Security number may have to show identification or proof of residency the first time they vote.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Identification', field CaliforniaID

fields.name: hasNoCaliforniaId
fields.label: I do not have a California driver license or California identification card.
fields.description: Gates californiaId's requiredness. Checking this and hasNoSsn together is accepted by the wizard (the source site states such applicants 'may still apply but must take additional steps to complete your voter registration').
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Identification', field HasNoCaliforniaID

fields.name: ssnLast4
fields.label: Social security number (last 4 numbers)
fields.description: Last four digits only. Required unless hasNoSsn is checked.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Identification', field SSN4

fields.name: hasNoSsn
fields.label: I do not have a social security number.
fields.description: Gates ssnLast4's requiredness. See californiaId/hasNoCaliforniaId's description for the combined no-ID, no-SSN case.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information' — 'Identification', field HasNoSSN

fields.name: ethnicity
fields.label: My ethnicity/race is
fields.description: Optional self-reported ethnicity/race, collected for federal Voting Rights Act compliance reporting (not printed on this page, but standard practice for this data element).
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field EthnicityId

fields.name: languagePreference
fields.label: My language preference for receiving election materials is
fields.description: Preferred language for election materials.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field LanguagePreferenceId

fields.name: otherLanguage
fields.label: Other Language
fields.description: Free-text language name, collected when languagePreference is 'other'. Modelled here per the field's evident intent; the live server was observed to reject a submission that left this field blank even when languagePreference was 'english', which this schema treats as a form implementation quirk rather than the field's real requiredness rule — see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Personal Information', field OtherLanguage

fields.name: homeAddressChoice
fields.label: Home Address
fields.description: Whether the applicant has a conventional street address or must instead describe their residence (e.g. an unhoused applicant using cross-streets).
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field HomeAddressChoice — 'I have a street address.' / 'I do not have a street address.'

fields.name: homeStreetAddress
fields.label: Street address
fields.description: Residential street address; not a P.O. Box or business address. Must clearly identify the residence, per the form.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field Home.StreetAddress

fields.name: homeApartmentOrUnitNumber
fields.label: Apartment or unit number
fields.description: Optional apartment or unit number for the home street address.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field Home.ApartmentLotNumber

fields.name: homeCity
fields.label: City
fields.description: City of the home street address. The home address state is fixed to California by the source form and is not itself a collected field.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field Home.City

fields.name: homeZip
fields.label: Zip
fields.description: 5-digit ZIP code of the home street address; the source form range-validates it between 90001 and 96162 (California's ZIP code range).
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field Home.Zip

fields.name: homeNoStreetAddressDescription
fields.label: Describe where you live including cross streets, Route, N, S, E, W
fields.description: Free-text description of the applicant's residence location, used instead of a conventional street address.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field Home.NoStreetAddress

fields.name: homeNoStreetAddressCity
fields.label: City
fields.description: City corresponding to the no-street-address description.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field Home.NoAddressCity

fields.name: homeCounty
fields.label: County
fields.description: County of residence. The source presents a fixed 58-item dropdown (California's own counties); modelled as free text per this registry's large-geographic-dropdown convention rather than embedding the full option list as an enum.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field Home.CountyId

fields.name: isMailingAddressDifferent
fields.label: My mailing address is different from my home address.
fields.description: Gates the mailing-address fields; leave unchecked if mail should go to the home address.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field IsMailingAddressDifferent

fields.name: mailingStreetAddress
fields.label: Street address or P.O. Box
fields.description: Mailing street address or P.O. Box, if different from the home address. Unlike the home address, a P.O. Box is accepted here.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Mailing Address', field Mailing.StreetAddress

fields.name: mailingApartmentOrUnitNumber
fields.label: Apartment or unit number
fields.description: Optional apartment or unit number for the mailing address.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Mailing Address', field Mailing.ApartmentLotNumber

fields.name: mailingCity
fields.label: City
fields.description: City of the mailing address.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Mailing Address', field Mailing.City

fields.name: mailingState
fields.label: State
fields.description: State of the mailing address (not fixed to California, unlike the home address). The source presents the same fixed 59-item state/territory dropdown as statePlaceOfBirth; modelled as free text for the same reason.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Mailing Address', field Mailing.StateId

fields.name: mailingZip
fields.label: Zip
fields.description: ZIP code of the mailing address; unlike homeZip, not range-restricted to California.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Mailing Address', field Mailing.Zip

fields.name: isPreviouslyRegistered
fields.label: I was previously registered or pre-registered to vote.
fields.description: Gates the previous-registration recall fields below, used to help election officials locate an existing record (e.g. after a name or address change). None of the previous-registration fields are themselves server-enforced as required even when this is checked — they help officials find a prior record but are not a hard prerequisite.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address', field IsPreviouslyRegistered

fields.name: previousFirstName
fields.label: First name
fields.description: First name the applicant was previously registered under, if known.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.FirstName

fields.name: previousMiddleName
fields.label: Middle name
fields.description: Middle name the applicant was previously registered under, if known.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.MiddleName

fields.name: previousLastName
fields.label: Last name
fields.description: Last name the applicant was previously registered under, if known.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.LastName

fields.name: previousStreetAddress
fields.label: Street address
fields.description: Street address the applicant was previously registered at, if known.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.StreetAddress

fields.name: previousApartmentOrUnitNumber
fields.label: Apartment or unit number
fields.description: Apartment or unit number of the previous address, if known.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.ApartmentLotNumber

fields.name: previousCity
fields.label: City
fields.description: City of the previous address, if known.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.City

fields.name: previousState
fields.label: State
fields.description: State of the previous address, if known. Presents the same fixed 59-item dropdown as statePlaceOfBirth/mailingState; modelled as free text for the same reason.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.StateId

fields.name: previousZip
fields.label: Zip
fields.description: ZIP code of the previous address, if known.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.Zip

fields.name: previousPoliticalParty
fields.label: Previous political party preference (if any)
fields.description: Free-text previous political party preference, if known — distinct from the current politicalParty field below, which uses a fixed, closed list.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Home and Mailing Address' — 'Previous Registration', field Previous.PoliticalParty

fields.name: politicalPartyPreferenceChoice
fields.label: Do you want to choose a political party preference?
fields.description: Whether the applicant registers with a party preference or as No Party/None. Choosing No Party/None may restrict which parties' candidates the voter can select in a primary election for U.S. President, or for a party's central committee.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Political Party Preference', field PoliticalPreferenceType

fields.name: politicalParty
fields.label: Yes, my political party preference is
fields.description: Selected political party, when politicalPartyPreferenceChoice is has_preference.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Political Party Preference', field PoliticalPartyId

fields.name: otherPoliticalParty
fields.label: Other (specify)
fields.description: Free-text party name, when politicalParty is other_party.
fields.sourceRef: Step 2 'Political Party Preference', field OtherPoliticalParty

fields.name: wantsStateVoterGuideByMail
fields.label: I want to get my state voter information guide by mail before each statewide election
fields.description: If everyone in the applicant's household opts out, no state voter information guide is mailed to that address; the guide remains available online, and any other household member's opt-in still results in a mailed copy for the address.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'State Voter Information Guide', field IsVIG

fields.name: wantsCountyVoterGuideByMail
fields.label: I want to get my county voter information guide by mail before each election.
fields.description: Opting out results in electronic notification instead, where the applicant's county supports it; otherwise the county voter information guide is mailed regardless.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'County Voter Information Guide', field IsSampleBallot

fields.name: electronicNotificationEmail
fields.label: Electronic notification email address
fields.description: Optional email address for electronic notification when a county voter information guide is available online instead of by mail.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Electronic Notification by email', field NotificationEmailAddress

fields.name: confirmElectronicNotificationEmail
fields.label: Confirm electronic notification email
fields.description: Repeat of electronicNotificationEmail for confirmation.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Electronic Notification by email', field NotificationConfirmEmail

fields.name: wantsToBePollWorker
fields.label: I want to be a poll worker and assist voters on election day.
fields.description: Gates spokenLanguages and canProvidePollingPlace.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Volunteer on Election Day', field CanBePollWorker

fields.name: spokenLanguages
fields.label: The languages I speak are
fields.description: Select-all-that-apply. Modelled as a comma-separated list of: english, chinese, japanese, korean, spanish, tagalog, vietnamese, hindi, khmer, thai, other — the same multi-select-as-string treatment as us/uscis/travel-document-i131's race field, pending the GSP-0009 repeating/multi-select gap.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Volunteer on Election Day', field SpokenLanguageIds

fields.name: otherSpokenLanguage
fields.label: Other spoken language
fields.description: Free-text language name, meaningful only when spokenLanguages includes 'other' — a sub-condition on a comma-joined field the shared Condition grammar cannot express directly, so it is documented here in prose rather than encoded (the same class of gap as au/aec/voter-enrolment's citizenshipCertificateNumber exemption).
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Volunteer on Election Day', field OtherSpokenLanguages

fields.name: canProvidePollingPlace
fields.label: I can provide a polling place on election day.
fields.description: Offer of a physical location to serve as a polling place.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Volunteer on Election Day', field CanProvidePollingPlace

fields.name: wantsAccessibleMaterialFormat
fields.label: I want voting materials in an accessible format
fields.description: Request for voting materials in an accessible format.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Accessibility', field IsAccessibleMaterialRequired

fields.name: receivedThirdPartyHelp
fields.label: Did someone help you fill out this form?
fields.description: Gates the third-party helper fields. The form's own instructions state the helper 'must fill out the questions below' if yes, but no server-side required validation was observed on the helper fields themselves.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Help Filling Out This Form', field ReceivedThirdPartyHelp.ReceivedThirdPartyHelp

fields.name: thirdPartyHelperName
fields.label: Name
fields.description: Name of the person who helped complete the form. Personal information of a third party; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Help Filling Out This Form', field ReceivedThirdPartyHelp.ThirdPartyPersonName

fields.name: thirdPartyHelperAddress
fields.label: Address
fields.description: Address of the person who helped complete the form. Personal information of a third party; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Help Filling Out This Form', field ReceivedThirdPartyHelp.ThirdPartyAddress

fields.name: thirdPartyHelperPhone
fields.label: Phone Number
fields.description: Phone number of the person who helped complete the form.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Help Filling Out This Form', field ReceivedThirdPartyHelp.ThirdPartyPhone

fields.name: thirdPartyHelperOrganization
fields.label: Organization name and telephone number (if any)
fields.description: Organization the helper is affiliated with, if any, and its telephone number.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Help Filling Out This Form', field ReceivedThirdPartyHelp.ThirdPartyOrganizationInfo

fields.name: affirmation
fields.label: I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that: I am a U.S. citizen and a resident of California and at least 18 years old; I am not currently serving a state or federal prison term for the conviction of a felony; I am not currently found mentally incompetent to vote by a court; I understand that it is a crime to intentionally provide incorrect information on this form; the information on this form is true and correct.
fields.description: Final penalty-of-perjury affirmation and eligibility declaration; must be checked to submit. Applicants pre-registering at 16-17 are not yet held to the 'at least 18 years old' clause in practice, though the form presents one combined affirmation checkbox for both registrant and pre-registrant paths.
fields.sourceRef: Step 3 'Affirmation', field Affirmation

steps.title: Volunteer on election day and accessibility
